title: Werauhia cowellii
intro: Werauhia cowellii, also known as the "Resurrection Plant," is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family (Bromeliaceae) that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia cowellii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Werauhia cowellii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It belongs to the genus Werauhia, which is a group of tropical plants native to Central and South America. The Bromeliaceae family consists of over 3,000 species of plants, including pineapples, air plants, and orchids. Werauhia cowellii is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within this family. Further classification of Werauhia cowellii includes its order (Pohlioales), class (Liliopsida), and division (Angiospermophyta).
